sql_injection.rule: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. NEVER use string formatting (e.g., f-strings, %s, .format()) to construct SQL queries with user input.

command_injection.rule: Use subprocess.run() or subprocess.Popen() with a list of arguments and shell=False. Never pass unsanitized user input to a shell command.

path_traversal.rule: Sanitize file paths using os.path.basename() or secure path resolution (e.g., resolving absolute paths and checking if they start with the expected base directory).

xss.rule: Escape HTML content before rendering it. Use contextual output encoding (e.g., html.escape() in Python or a template engine with auto-escaping).

insecure_deserialization.rule: Use safe serialization formats like JSON. If using YAML, use yaml.safe_load() instead of yaml.load(). Never use pickle with untrusted data.

hardcoded_secrets.rule: Load secrets from environment variables (os.getenv) or a secure secrets manager. Do not hardcode credentials in the source code.

ssrf.rule: Validate all URLs against an allowlist of permitted domains or IP ranges. Do not allow requests to internal network resources (e.g., 127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254).

xxe.rule: Disable external entity resolution when parsing XML. In Python, use defusedxml instead of the standard xml library.
